title: angelica japonica var. hirsutiflora
intro: Angelica japonica var. hirsutiflora, also known as Japanese angelica, is a plant species that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is a perennial herb native to East Asia, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese and Japanese medicine. The plant's roots and rhizomes are used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. Angelica japonica var. hirsutiflora is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and its health benefits also include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and alleviate menstrual cramps.
